cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Workato Launches Enterprise MCP Registry for Governed AI Capability Discovery
stories.plain: Workato's new Enterprise MCP Registry gives large organisations a single, governed catalogue of AI tools and agent capabilities — covering who can use them, how, and under what rules. As companies deploy more autonomous agents across marketing and ops, having a governed registry to discover and control those agents is quickly becoming a buying requirement, not a nice-to-have.

stories.head: 91% of Marketers Claim an AI Search Strategy but Most Lack Real Execution, Survey Finds
stories.plain: A 600-plus-person survey by Scribewise and Scrunch exposes a sharp gap: nearly all marketers say they have a Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) strategy, but 62% admit they're already behind competitors in actual execution. The data signals that GEO is shifting from experimental to a core budget line — and most teams are underprepared.

stories.head: Marchex Completes Acquisition of Archenia to Build AI-Driven Customer Acquisition Platform
stories.plain: Marchex has closed its purchase of Archenia, combining conversational intelligence analytics with AI-verified, outcome-based lead qualification. The merged entity targets the full funnel from intent to conversion — a structural shift in how AI is bought and bundled for performance marketing, moving beyond analytics into active acquisition decisioning.

stories.head: Forrester's Chuck Gahun: AI Agents Are a New Persona Requiring Their Own Content Strategy
stories.plain: Forrester analyst Chuck Gahun argues that GEO is not just updated SEO — AI agents retrieving information for users behave as a distinct audience with different content needs, evaluation criteria, and trust signals. Marketers who treat GEO as a tweak to existing SEO practices will structurally lose visibility inside AI-generated answers.

stories.head: Agentic AI ROI Reality Check: Most Deployments Fail to Deliver Tangible Enterprise Benefits
stories.plain: A sober analysis finds that despite enormous excitement, the vast majority of agentic AI deployments have not produced measurable ROI, and no publicly known case of enterprise-wide adoption exists. For buyers evaluating agentic marketing tools, this sets realistic expectations and raises hard questions about governance, scope, and vendor accountability before signing contracts.

stories.head: 5 Questions to Ask AI Vendors Before You Buy Anything
stories.plain: A practical buyer's framework asking vendors about business value proof, data policies, and real customer evidence before signing. For anyone new to MarTech-AI purchasing, this is a direct guide to avoiding expensive mistakes — cutting through vendor superlatives with concrete evaluation criteria that apply at any budget level.

stories.head: Workato Launches Enterprise MCP Registry for Governed AI Capability Management at Scale
stories.plain: Workato's new Enterprise MCP Registry gives large organizations a single governed catalogue for discovering, securing, and controlling AI agent capabilities across systems. This matters because ungoverned AI tool sprawl is a top enterprise risk — a centralized registry directly shapes how agentic AI is bought, audited, and kept compliant.

stories.head: Survey: 91% of Marketers Claim an AI Search Strategy, But Most Are Far Behind on Execution
stories.plain: A 600-person survey finds a stark gap: nearly all marketers say they have a Generative Engine Optimization strategy, yet 62% admit they're already losing ground to competitors. The data signals that GEO is moving from optional to mandatory, and most teams lack the content infrastructure to execute it.

stories.head: Forrester's Chuck Gahun on Marketing to Machines and the Rise of the AI Agent Persona
stories.plain: Forrester analyst Chuck Gahun explains why GEO is not just extended SEO — AI agents are a distinct audience with their own content needs and decision logic. Brands must now structure content so autonomous agents can find, trust, and cite them. This reframes how content strategy is planned and budgeted.

stories.head: Looking Beyond the Hype: Why Most Agentic AI Deployments Fail to Deliver Real ROI
stories.plain: A frank assessment noting that virtually no enterprise has achieved company-wide agentic AI ROI — deployments stay stuck inside single functions or business units. For buyers evaluating agentic platforms, this sets realistic expectations and highlights the governance and integration gaps that must be solved before scale is possible.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: Agentic AI moves from hype to infrastructure
body: The wave of agent-native launches — Workato's Enterprise MCP Registry, Revid.ai's MCP Server, Hexaware's agent-native development partnership, and Hermes Agent Desktop — signals that agentic AI is no longer a demo feature but a foundational layer. SaaS vendors that cannot embed autonomous execution into their platforms are already losing positioning ground.

For decision-makers, this means the buying question has shifted: it is no longer 'does this tool use AI?' but 'can an agent orchestrate it end-to-end without human hand-holding?' Vendors that expose agent-compatible APIs & registries are setting the new interoperability standard.
ev: MarTech · SaaS can no longer compete on software alone

themes.head: AI search strategy: claimed but hollow
body: New research finds 91% of marketers say they have an AI search strategy, yet the vast majority have done little beyond surface-level adjustments. The gap between claimed readiness and actual execution is the defining credibility risk of the moment — teams that mistake tactic for strategy will be blindsided as AI-mediated search reshapes discovery.

Compounding this, the CMS is quietly becoming the AI operating system for brands, meaning content architecture decisions made today directly determine how well AI agents surface a brand tomorrow. Marketers who treat AI search as an SEO add-on rather than a structural rethink will fall behind competitors who reorganize content pipelines around machine readability.
ev: MarTech Series · 91% claim AI search strategy
MarTech · CMS as AI OS for brands
Agile Brand · Marketing to Machines with Forrester

themes.head: Data infrastructure is the real AI bottleneck
body: Multiple signals this month converge on a single constraint: most organizations' data foundations are not ready to power the AI ambitions sitting above them. AI-ready infrastructure is hard to build not because tools are missing, but because data quality, governance, and integration debt compound every new AI initiative. The DataTerrain BI migration story and the MMM open-source accessibility piece both underscore that cheaper tooling does not eliminate the underlying complexity.

Marketing performance measurement suffers the same fracture — ad spend is routinely confused with true marketing performance, and without clean, unified data, AI models simply amplify existing blind spots. Decision-makers must treat data infrastructure as a prerequisite investment, not an afterthought, before committing to additional AI tooling.
ev: MarTech · Don't confuse ad spend with performance
MarTech · Open source made MMM cheaper not easier

themes.head: The human edge in an automated CX era
body: As AI adoption in CX crosses 90% and deployment paths diverge rapidly, a counter-narrative is gaining traction: the differentiator competitors cannot buy is the quality of human judgment, empathy, and relationship-building layered on top of automation. Gen Z data adds a sharp twist — the cohort most comfortable with AI is simultaneously the most vocal about its risks, signalling that trust cannot be automated away.

For marketing leaders, the practical implication is a workforce & skills question as much as a technology one. If AI handles executional work, junior marketers lose the repetitive-task training ground that builds expertise — raising an urgent question about how the next generation of strategists will be developed inside organizations that have automated their way through entry-level work.
ev: Agile Brand · Rachel Orston on human edge in CX
Agile Brand · Gen Z embracing AI & sounding alarm

themes.head: Consolidation reshaping the MarTech stack
body: A cluster of acquisitions — Marchex absorbing Archenia, Brunner acquiring AdSkate, Creatio targeting the enterprise 'Frankenstack' — points to a deliberate market move toward fewer, more integrated platforms. Vendors are betting that buyers are exhausted by stitching together point solutions and will pay a premium for unified intelligence. The AI angle is central: each deal explicitly extends AI capability rather than simply adding headcount or reach.

For buyers, this consolidation pressure changes vendor evaluation criteria. The five questions to ask AI vendors before purchasing matter more than ever: lock-in risk, data ownership, and agent-compatibility are now as important as feature parity. Organizations that delay rationalizing their stack will find migration costs rising as platforms entrench agent-native architectures.
ev: MarTech · 5 questions to ask AI vendors

month.takeaway: Across every signal this month, the real dividing line is not who has adopted AI but who has rebuilt their data, stack, and skills foundation to make AI execution actually work.
